aarav.name: Aarav Mehta
aarav.story: Aarav has 14 weeks until the TCS NQT window opens. He codes a little, knows enough SQL to be dangerous, and has never finished a DSA sheet. Budget for prep: zero rupees.
script_notes: Skill-DNA shows Python as inferred-strong, DSA and aptitude as weak-to-unknown - the gap map should feel earned, not generic.
Target path should land near 0.7 feasibility: achievable, not comfortable.
Week-1 quiz is scripted to score 60 percent so the Coach agent has a real trigger to react to.
Week-2 'missed week' is the catch-up replanning centrepiece - critical-path topics protected, optional ones deferred.

priya.name: Priya Raman
priya.story: Priya already codes well and wants the Capgemini Exceller process specifically. Ten weeks, twenty hours a week, and a small budget for one paid resource if it earns its place.
script_notes: Strong base profile - most foundation nodes should resolve to known/inferred quickly, leaving headroom for the Stretch path.
Stretch path should clear feasibility with advanced DSA and System Design surfacing in week one.
Good contrast persona for 'the system does not pad a strong learner's plan with busywork'.

rohan.name: Rohan Verma
rohan.tagline: MCA graduate, re-skilling toward Data Analyst roles
rohan.story: Rohan finished his MCA without a strong CS core. He is comfortable in Excel, can write a SQL query, and wants a Data Analyst seat at TCS. Twenty weeks, eight hours a week, zero budget.
script_notes: Background = non_cs should switch on the Re-Skiller Bridge framing - Python and core-CS bridge nodes inserted ahead of the standard Data Analyst chain.
Long runway (20 weeks) at low weekly hours (8) is the realism test: the safe path should be the one flagged feasible.
Good persona for showing the system *not* recommending Deep Learning before someone can write a for-loop.

sneha.name: Sneha Iyer
sneha.story: Sneha can already read a regression table better than most of her batch, but she has never written a script anyone else had to run. Eighteen weeks until the InfyTQ window, seventeen hours a week, and a small budget for one course that earns its place.
script_notes: Statistics rated 4 should ripple into inferred-known coverage on the Data & Intelligence axis before a single hour of new study - the gap map should visibly credit what she already has.

Data Structures & Algorithms at 1 is the honest tension in this persona: a Data Scientist track still opens through the same programming-foundation nodes an SDE track does, and the plan should say so plainly rather than skip ahead to modelling.
Good persona for surfacing the MLOps-to-cloud-fundamentals prerequisite the validator's checks were written to confirm - her path runs straight through it.

vikram.name: Vikram Nair
vikram.tagline: Diploma-to-B.E. lateral entry, Tier-3 college, Indore
vikram.story: Vikram builds landing pages for two shops in his neighbourhood and was fairly sure that counted as 'full stack' until a friend asked what runs behind the contact form. Twelve weeks to the Wipro NLTH window, eighteen hours a week, no budget for courses.
script_notes: JavaScript at 3 should not over-credit backend depth - the gap map should still show real ground on the server and database side, exactly the blind spot in his own self-description.
Free budget plus a twelve-week runway makes this the tightest feasibility story of the five - the safe path doing real, visible work is the point of including it.
Diploma-entry background is the last of the four onboarding 'background' values the demo set had not yet exercised.
